title: Index Multiple Selection
summary: Feature parent for explicit checkbox selection and group-move work, separated from its shipped action-target owner.
content_html:
Index Multiple Selection

Purpose

Add explicit checkbox selection to the manage-mode index, then let deliberately designed batch actions consume that selection without changing the displayed document.

This is the feature parent. It does not act as an implementation tracker.

Feature Documents

  • Concept — desired interaction, product boundaries, and deferred choices.
  • Architecture — proposed state, ownership, action-target, drag/drop, and service boundaries.
  • Delivery — active checkbox-selection foundation, reload behavior, and bounded implementation checkpoints.
  • Docs Viewer Roadmap — scheduling, dependencies, and suggested feature order.

Current State

The action-target owner is complete. docs-viewer-action-definitions.js gives current controls one code-owned target resolver, and the first delivery checkpoint now supplies an independent checked-document state owner without changing current action targets. Runtime Module Ownership is the durable owner for that shipped boundary.

The selection state is not visible yet. IMS-2 remains the separately gated checkbox-control and row-projection checkpoint.

Checkbox controls, group drag/drop, plural move service behavior, and additional multi-document consumers are not implemented.

The active delivery is bounded to the visible checkbox-selection foundation. Group movement and Document Sharing UI Redesign remain separately gated follow-ons, so this delivery document does not imply that either is implemented or authorized.
